title: The Eumerella Shore
content:
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s a happy little valley on the Eumerella shore,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Where Iï؟½ve lingered many happy hours away,
آ آ آ آ On my little free selection I have acres by the score,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Where I unyoke the bullocks from the dray.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  To my bullocks then I say
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  No matter where you stray,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  You will never be impounded any more;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  For youï؟½re running, running, running on the dufferï؟½s piece of land,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Free selected on the Eumerella shore.

آ آ آ آ When the moon has climbed the mountains and the stars are shining bright,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Then we saddle up our horses and away,
آ آ آ آ And we yard the squattersï؟½ cattle in the darkness of the night,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And we have the calves all branded by the day.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ Oh, my pretty little calf,
آ آ آ آ At the squatter you may laugh,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For heï؟½ll never be your owner any more;
آ آ آ آ For youï؟½re running, running, running on the dufferï؟½s piece of land,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Free selected on the Eumerella shore.

آ آ آ آ If we find a mob of horses when the paddock rails are down,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Although before theyï؟½re never known to stray,
آ آ آ آ Oh, quickly will we drive them to some distant inland town,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And sell them into slavï؟½ry far away.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  To Jack Robertson weï؟½ll say
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Youï؟½ve been leading us astray,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  And weï؟½ll never go a-farming any more;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  For itï؟½s easier duffing cattle on the little piece of land
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Free selected on the Eumerella shore.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
